about.title: About
about.description: About Hilal Vision - a precision astronomical platform for predicting Islamic crescent moon sightings worldwide using Yallop & Odeh criteria.
about.heroDesc1: Hilal Vision is a precision astronomical web platform dedicated to predicting and visualising Islamic crescent moon (
about.heroDesc3: ) sightings worldwide. It is built for the 1.8 billion Muslims who rely on the lunar calendar for religious observances, and for the astronomers and scholars who study it.
about.missionP1: The Islamic lunar calendar is one of humanity's oldest scientific traditions - the beginning of each sacred month has been determined by the physical sighting of the new crescent moon for over 1,400 years. Yet in the modern world, this practice is fragmented: different countries announce the start of Ramadan on different days, and communities lack transparent, data-driven tools to understand why.
about.missionP2_1: Hilal Vision exists to answer one question with the precision it deserves:
about.missionP2_2: "Will the new crescent moon be visible tonight from my location - and why?"
about.missionP3_5: visibility criteria - the same standards used by Islamic calendar authorities in the UK, Malaysia, and the international astronomical community - and present the results with the visual clarity and interactivity that a 21st-century audience expects.
about.missionP4: This platform is not a religious authority. It is a scientific instrument. It presents mathematical predictions, historical data, and comparative calendrical analysis side-by-side, empowering individuals and communities to engage critically and transparently with Islamic timekeeping.
about.whoMuslimsDesc: Get a clear, location-specific prediction for crescent visibility before Ramadan, Eid, and every new Hijri month - without jargon.
about.whoAstroDesc: Access raw q-values, ARCV/DAZ parameters, Odeh V-values, and the full ICOP historical dataset for validation and research.
about.whoScholarsDesc: Compare Astronomical, Umm al-Qura, and Tabular Hijri calendars side-by-side. Understand exactly where and why civic calendars diverge from physical astronomy.
about.toolsDesc: Six dedicated tools, each engineered for a specific aspect of lunar astronomy.
about.tools_visDesc: Interactive WebGL globe and 2D Leaflet map showing crescent visibility zones worldwide using Yallop q-values. Includes real-time cloud cover overlay and Best-Time-to-Observe calculator.
about.tools_moonDesc: Current lunar phase, illumination, age, Sun & Moon altitude chart, 30-day phase calendar strip, and Yallop/Danjon methodology charts.
about.tools_calDesc: Triple-engine calendar supporting Astronomical (astronomy-engine), Umm al-Qura (KACST), and Tabular algorithms. Includes a 'Compare to Heavens' divergence overlay.
about.tools_horDesc: Local horizon simulator showing the crescent moon's position relative to the setting sun, with ARCV and DAZ annotations.
about.tools_arcDesc: 1,000+ authentic historical crescent sighting records from the Islamic Crescents' Observation Project (ICOP), spanning 1438–1465 AH.
about.techDesc: Built with modern web technologies for performance, accuracy, and global reach.
about.tech_astro: VSOP87/ELP2000 sun & moon position algorithms (Don Cross)
about.tech_trpc: Type-safe API and database layer (Drizzle ORM)
about.tech_sentry: Error monitoring and offline service worker
about.deepDiveDesc: Read the full technical documentation - Yallop and Odeh criteria derivations, triple-engine Hijri calendar algorithms, the Best-Time-to-Observe scoring function, atmospheric refraction physics, and ICOP data sourcing.
about.compareDesc: No competitor combines all of: 3D globe, weather overlay, real ICOP data, and a Best-Time-to-Observe engine. Hilal Vision uniquely owns this combination.
about.tableNote: Table reflects publicly available features as of February 2026. ✓ = available, - = not available.
about.creditsDesc: Hilal Vision stands on the shoulders of decades of peer-reviewed astronomical research.
about.credit_yallopDesc: B.D. Yallop, HM Nautical Almanac Office - foundational q-value crescent visibility formula.
about.credit_odehDesc: Mohammad Odeh - V-value refinement trained on 737 ICOP sighting observations.
about.credit_icopDesc: International Astronomical Center - over 1,000 historical crescent sighting records used in the Archive.
about.credit_astroDesc: High-precision VSOP87/ELP2000 planetary and lunar position library for JavaScript.
about.credit_ummDesc: KACST pre-computed tables for the official Saudi Arabian civic Hijri calendar.
about.credit_meteoDesc: Free, open-source weather API providing real-time cloud cover data.
about.licenseDesc: Hilal Vision is proprietary software. All rights are reserved. The source code is closed-source and protected by copyright law.
about.contactDesc: Found a bug, have a question about the methodology, or want to contribute sighting data? We welcome feedback from the astronomical and Islamic community.

visibility.title: Visibility - 3D Globe & 2D Map
visibility.description: Interactive crescent moon visibility predictions on a 3D globe and 2D map with cloud cover overlay and best observation time calculator.

calendar.subtitle: Interactive Islamic date converter & event tracker

A.label: Easily Visible
A.desc: The moon is high and thick enough at sunset that seeing it with the naked eye is highly probable.

B.label: Visible
B.desc: Sightings are possible with the naked eye, but usually require perfect weather conditions.

C.label: Optical Aid Helpful
C.desc: You will likely need binoculars or a telescope to find the moon initially.

D.label: Optical Aid Only
D.desc: The moon is too faint or low; telescopes or binoculars are strictly required to see it.

E.label: Not Visible
E.desc: Slightly above the horizon, but below the threshold of human or optical visibility.

F.label: Below Horizon
F.desc: Moon sets before or simultaneously with the sun, or conjunction hasn't occurred yet.

support.heroDesc: Hilal Vision serves 1.8 billion Muslims who rely on the lunar calendar. Your support — whether through Pro or a one-time contribution — helps keep the platform running, accurate, and ad-free. In the Islamic tradition, supporting beneficial knowledge is considered
support.keepRunningDesc: Server costs, API calls, weather data, and real-time ephemeris computation — science isn't free.
support.keepAccurateDesc: Continuous calibration against ICOP observations, new criterion research, and multi-engine calendar validation.
support.keepFreeDesc: Hilal Vision aims to be a clean, respectful experience. Your support replaces the need for intrusive advertising.
support.freeVsProDesc: Every feature is visible from Day 1. Pro unlocks deeper interaction and advanced tools.
support.planSubtitle: Unlock every feature. Support Islamic astronomy.
support.donationDesc: Not ready for Pro? A one-time donation of any amount helps cover server costs and keeps Hilal Vision freely accessible to communities worldwide. Donors who give $10+ receive a
support.donationPatron: patron badge on their sighting reports.
support.secureStripe: Secure payment via Stripe · 🔒 End-to-end encrypted
support.welcomePro: Welcome to Hilal Vision Pro! جزاك الله خيراً 🌙
support.thanksDonation: Thank you for your donation! جزاك الله خيراً 🤲
support.paymentCancelled: Payment canceled — no charge was made.

mapLegend.title: How to Read This Map
mapLegend.compositeDesc1: The map does not show a simultaneous snapshot. Instead, it calculates the moon's visibility at the <1>exact local time of sunset</1> for every point on Earth. Because sunset sweeps from East to West over 24 hours, the map is a mosaic of those individual sunset moments stitched together.
mapLegend.compositeDesc2: If the world is green (Zone A) today, it broadly means: when the sun sets in Australia, the moon is easily visible. Hours later when the sun sets in the Middle East, it's visible. And later in the Americas, it's still visible.
mapLegend.criterionDesc: Based on the <1>{{criterion}}</1> criterion, representing the probability of sighting at local sunset:
mapLegend.quote: "If I stand at this location today, what are my chances of seeing the new moon when the sun sets here locally?"

proGate.upgradeDesc2: for full access to advanced tools and analytics.

physicsExplanations.title: Scientific & Astronomical Methodology
physicsExplanations.yallopDesc1: The Hilal Dashboard employs the rigorous <1>Yallop (1997) criterion</1> to predict lunar crescent visibility. Dr. B.D. Yallop of the HM Nautical Almanac Office developed this statistical model based on 295 historical observations.
physicsExplanations.yallopARCVDesc: : Arc of Vision (Altitude difference between Moon and Sun).
physicsExplanations.yallopDesc2: The <1>q-value</1> elegantly predicts visibility across specific zones ranging from naked-eye certain (Zone A) to wholly invisible (Zone F).
physicsExplanations.odehDesc1: The <1>Odeh (2004) criterion</1> was developed by Mohammad Sh. Odeh of the Islamic Crescents' Observation Project (ICOP), refining Yallop's model with 737 observations including modern CCD and telescopic sightings.
physicsExplanations.odehV1Desc: : Crescent easily visible to the naked eye.
physicsExplanations.odehV3Desc: : May need optical aid to locate the crescent.
physicsExplanations.odehV4Desc: : Not visible, even with optical aid.
physicsExplanations.danjonDesc1: In 1932, French astronomer André Danjon discovered that sighting a crescent is physically impossible if the lunar elongation (angular distance between the sun and moon) is less than <1>7 degrees</1>.
physicsExplanations.danjonDesc2: At an elongation of exactly 7°, the shadowed mountains on the lunar surface entirely occlude the lit valleys, rendering the crescent discontinuous and invisible from Earth. Our engine enforces this limit implicitly through the Phase Angle bounds within the Yallop functions.
physicsExplanations.refractionDesc1: Our geometry computations via <1>astronomy-engine</1> (VSOP87/ELP2000) account for standard atmospheric refraction — which "lifts" objects above the true horizon.
physicsExplanations.refractionDesc2: True Altitude (H_true) is calculated geometrically relative to the center of the Earth. Refracted Altitude (H_app) factors in the bending of light through the Earth's atmosphere, which is largest precisely when viewing objects like the Hilal hugging the horizon.
physicsExplanations.refractionNote: Note: The web-app defaults to calculation at 0m elevation. Mountain sighting ranges require adjusting the geographic dip of the horizon.

terms.title: Terms of Service
terms.description: Hilal Vision Terms of Service - acceptable use, liability, and End-User License Agreement.
terms.intro1: These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern your use of Hilal Vision ("the Platform"). By accessing or using the Platform you agree to these Terms. If you do not agree, please do not use the Platform.
terms.p1: By using Hilal Vision - whether as a guest or a registered account holder - you confirm that you have read, understood, and agree to be bound by these Terms and our Privacy Policy.
terms.p2: Hilal Vision is an astronomical tool designed to calculate and visualise crescent moon visibility based on Yallop and Odeh criteria. It features:
terms.p2_1: Hilal Vision is a scientific calculation platform. It is
terms.p2_2: a religious authority. The platform provides astronomical possibilities, not legal or authoritative religious declarations of the Islamic month start. Users should continue to consult their local authorities and scholars.
terms.use1: Use the Platform for any unlawful purpose.
terms.use2: Attempt to probe, scan, or test the vulnerability of the service or breach any security or authentication measures.
terms.use3: Interfere with, or attempt to interfere with, the access of any user, host, or network, including sending a virus, overloading, or spamming.
terms.use4: Use automated systems (bots, scrapers) to extract data from the Platform without prior written consent.
terms.use5: Upload false, misleading, or malicious sighting reports to the public map.
terms.use6: Sell, resell, or commercially exploit any part of the service.
terms.use_outro: We reserve the right to suspend or terminate your account and block IP addresses if you violate these rules.
terms.content1: When you submit a crescent sighting report (including text, GPS coordinates, and photos), you grant us a worldwide, non-exclusive, royalty-free licence to use, reproduce, display, and distribute that data publicly as part of our scientific sighting repository.
terms.content2: You warrant that any reports you submit are truthful to the best of your knowledge and do not infringe on any third-party rights.
terms.acc1: Authentication is provided via Clerk. You are responsible for safeguarding your login credentials (or third-party OAuth accounts like Google/Apple).
terms.acc2: We cannot and will not be liable for any loss or damage arising from your failure to protect your account.
terms.ip1: The Hilal Vision brand, logos, UI designs, code architecture, and proprietary data processing algorithms are the exclusive property of Hilal Vision. You may not copy, modify, distribute, or reverse engineer them.
terms.ip2_1: Historical sighting data belongs to the Islamic Crescents' Observation Project (ICOP) and is used with attribution. Third-party libraries (e.g., astronomy-engine) are used under their respective open-source licenses. See the
terms.liab1: Hilal Vision is provided on an "AS IS" and "AS AVAILABLE" basis. We make no warranties regarding the absolute accuracy of the astronomical predictions, Yallop/Odeh criteria outcomes, or weather/cloud-cover data.
terms.liab2: To the maximum extent permitted by law, Hilal Vision shall not be liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or punitive damages resulting from your use of or inability to use the service.
terms.third1: The Platform relies on external APIs (e.g., Open-Meteo for weather, Clerk for authentication, Vercel for hosting). We are not responsible for outages, inaccuracies, or changes in these third-party services.
terms.mod1: We reserve the right to modify these Terms at any time. Changes will be posted on this page with an updated date. Continued use of the Platform after changes constitutes acceptance of the new Terms.
terms.gov1: These Terms shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the jurisdiction in which the creators operate, without regard to its conflict of law provisions.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.description: Hilal Vision Privacy Policy - how we collect, use, and protect your data.
privacy.intro1: Hilal Vision ("we", "us", "our") is committed to protecting your privacy. This policy explains what data we collect, how we use it, and your rights regarding that data. By using Hilal Vision you agree to the practices described below.
privacy.intro2: If you have questions, contact us at
privacy.p1: We collect the minimum data necessary to provide the service:
privacy.collect_accountDesc: - Name and email address, collected by Clerk Auth when you sign in with Google, Apple, or email/password. We do not store passwords directly.
privacy.collect_locationDesc: - GPS coordinates you voluntarily provide when using the Auto-Detect feature or submitting a sighting report. Location is not stored continuously; it is used only at the moment of calculation or report submission.
privacy.collect_sightingDesc: - Observation time, GPS coordinates, sighting result (Seen / Not Seen), and optional notes that you submit voluntarily.
privacy.collect_usageDesc: - Anonymised event telemetry via Sentry (error reports, page performance metrics). No personally identifiable information is attached.
privacy.collect_ipDesc: - Temporarily logged by Upstash Redis for rate-limiting submitted sighting reports (sliding window, 5 requests/minute). Not stored permanently.
privacy.use1: To provide crescent visibility predictions for your location.
privacy.use2: To store and display your voluntary crescent sighting reports on the public map.
privacy.use3: To authenticate and manage your account securely via Clerk.
privacy.use4: To prevent abuse via IP-based rate limiting (Upstash Redis).
privacy.use5: To diagnose application errors and improve performance (Sentry).
privacy.p2_2: sell your data, use it for advertising, or share it with third parties beyond the sub-processors listed below.
privacy.cookie1: Hilal Vision uses only functional cookies - specifically the Clerk authentication session cookie required to keep you signed in. We do not use tracking or advertising cookies. Blocking cookies will prevent sign-in but will not affect astronomical calculations, which are fully client-side and require no account.
privacy.cookie2: If you access Hilal Vision from the European Union, the UK, or another jurisdiction that requires cookie consent, we will request your explicit consent before setting any cookies beyond strictly necessary ones.
privacy.retention_accountDesc: - Retained until you delete your account via Clerk.
privacy.retention_sightingDesc: - Retained indefinitely as part of the public scientific dataset. Reports you submit are attributed to your account and visible on the map.
privacy.retention_rateDesc: - IP entries expire automatically after 60 seconds.
privacy.rights_p1: Depending on your jurisdiction (GDPR, UK DPA 2018, CCPA, etc.), you may have rights to:
privacy.rights_1: Access the personal data we hold about you.
privacy.rights_3: Request deletion of your account and associated data.
privacy.rights_p2_1: To exercise any of these rights, contact
privacy.children1: Hilal Vision is not directed at children under 13. We do not knowingly collect personal data from children. If you believe a child has submitted data, contact us immediately.
privacy.changes1: We may update this policy from time to time. The "Last updated" date at the top of this page reflects the most recent revision. Significant changes will be announced via an in-app notification.
